rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN MALLOY VS. CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, December 23, 2024, Line 1, PLAINTIFF STEPHEN MALLOY's Hearing CC Section 1005. Off calendar for failure to provide courtesy copies in compliance with Local Rule 2.7B. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: 350 2ND STREET, LLC, VS. MH SAN FRAN HG PROPCO, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, December 23, 2024, Line 3, 2- CROSS-DEFENDANT 350 2ND STREET, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. Off calendar. The Law declaration indicates that the parties failed to meet and confer again after the First Amended Cross-Complaint was filed. CCP 435.5 indicates that if an amended pleading is filed, "the responding party shall meet and confer again with the party who filed the amended pleading before filing a motion to strike to the amended pleading." (CCP 435.5.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: BLANCA MENDEZ ET AL VS. ALBERTSONS COMPANIES, INC., A DELAWARE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Safeway, Inc.S Person(S) Most Qualified And Employees To Attend And Testify At A Deposition And To Produce The Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Monday, December 23, 2024, Line 1. 1 - PLAINTIFFs BLANCA MENDEZ, and O. M.'s Motion To Compel Defendant Safeway, Inc.S Person(S) Most Qualified And Employees To Attend And Testify At A Deposition And To Produce The Documents. Plaintiff Blanca Mendez's motion to compel deposition of Defendant Safeway, Inc.'s person(s) most qualified and employees and request for production of documents to which no opposition has been received is GRANTED. Plaintiff has demonstrated that the discovery at issue is are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Defendant Safeway, Inc. has not demonstrated a meritorious objection. Good cause appearing, the court orders Defendant SAFEWAY, INC. to produce the PMQs and employees as noticed and appear via videoconference at 8383 Wilshire Blvd., Ste. 822, Beverly Hills, California, within ten (10) days of this hearing, for their deposition, along with the production of documents as requested. Defendant Safeway, Inc.'s actions with respect to the subject discovery are without substantial justification. Defendant SAFEWAY, INC. and its counsel of record, Wolfe & Wyman, LLP, must pay sanctions in the amount of $1,200 to Plaintiff within ten (10) days of notice of this order. Plaintiff is advised courtesy copies must be properly fastened. Papers stapled in the middle of the page are not helpful and, in the future, may be disregarded. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)
